questions.question: Astronauts on the Moon see an asteroid crash into the surface. Why do they not hear the explosion?
options: Sound waves are unable to travel from the Moon's surface to the spaceship.
All the sound waves are absorbed by the surface of the Moon.
The sound waves are reflected from the surface of the spaceship.
The distance is too small for a sound wave to form.
questions.explanation:
Sound waves require a material medium. Since there is no air (vacuum) between the crash site and the ship, sound cannot travel. 

**Common mistake:** Believing the sound is absorbed by the Moon's surface.

questions.question: Using the data provided, which statement correctly describes the speed of sound?nn| Substance | Density ($g/cm^3$) | Speed of sound ($m/s$) |n| :--- | :--- | :--- |n| Lead | 11.3 | 1200 |n| Iron | 7.87 | 5000 |n| Oxygen | 0.00143 | 320 |n| Air | 0.00129 | 330 |
options: $Sound travels about 4 \times faster in iron than in lead$.
The denser the substance, the lower the speed of sound.
Sound travels fastest in oxygen.
Sound travels at the same speed in all gases.
questions.explanation:
Based on Table 14.2, speed in iron ($5000$) divided by speed in lead ($1200$) is $\approx 4.17$. 

**Common mistake:** Selecting B, even though lead is denser than iron but has a much lower sound speed.

questions.question: Observer A fires a flashgun, and Observer B, $500\text{ m}$ away, starts a stopwatch when she sees the flash. She stops it when she hears the sound $1.52\text{ s}$ later. What is the calculated speed of sound?
options: $329\text{ m/s}$
$330\text{ m/s}$
$760\text{ m/s}$
$340\text{ m/s}$
questions.explanation:
$\text{Speed} = \text{distance} / \text{time} = 500 / 1.52 \approx 329\text{ m/s}$. 

**Common mistake:** Assuming the speed is exactly $330\text{ m/s}$ without using the experimental data.

questions.question: In which of the following media will sound travel with the lowest speed?
options: Oxygen
Iron
Water
Lead
questions.explanation:
According to Table 14.2, sound travels slowest in oxygen ($320\text{ m/s}$), which is a gas. 

**Common mistake:** Thinking lead is the slowest because it is the densest solid listed.

questions.question: A ringing electric bell is suspended by a string inside a jar. A vacuum pump is used to remove the air. What is the observation and its reason?
options: Loudness decreases because sound needs a medium.
Loudness increases because there is no air to block it.
Pitch decreases because the frequency drops in a vacuum.
The bell stops vibrating because of the lack of air pressure.
questions.explanation:
As air is removed, the medium for sound transmission is lost, causing the loudness to decrease. 

**Common mistake:** Thinking the pitch changes or the bell stops vibrating.
